examples: Mr Crooke cites the anti-colonial writings of Frantz Fanon with enthusiasm, whereas those of free-market advocates are mocked and dismissed.
As the violence increased in Algeria, Fanon treated (mostly Arab) victims and (mostly French) perpetrators of torture who appealed to him for help.
David Macey's life of Fanon provides, as background, an excellent guide to the history of French decolonisation and the intellectual debates of post-war France.
